metadata_information.title: Borrowing to Keep Up (with the Joneses) : Inequality, Debt, and Conspicuous Consumption
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Borrowing to Keep Up (with the Joneses) : Inequality, Debt, and Conspicuous Consumption

document_description.abstract: The quest for status is a powerful motivator, but does it affect inequality? This paper presents a novel lab experiment that was designed and conducted to identify the relationship between inequality, status signaling, debt, and conspicuous consumption. It reports three main findings: First, consumption increases when it is "conspicuous" (i.e. is both observable, and signals ability/status). Second, borrowing increases when consumption is conspicuous. More critically, this increase in loan-taking is driven by those at the bottom of the income distribution. Third, in the presence of conspicuous consumption, access to finance exacerbates inequality. The results point to a vicious cycle of inequality and costly borrowing.

An objective of the series is to get the findings out quickly, even if the presentations are less than fully polished. The papers carry the names of the authors and should be cited accordingly.
